region_of_origin: Global — commercially farmed Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) originates primarily from Norway, Scotland, Chile, and Canada; widely sold in Singapore supermarkets
food_subgroup: Fatty fish, raw, skin-off — very high omega-3 content (~1.8g EPA+DHA per 100g raw)

main_ingredients: Atlantic salmon fillet (Salmo salar), commercially farmed, skin removed

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 100g raw skin-off Atlantic salmon fillet portion, equivalent to approximately one palm-sized piece as sold at Singapore supermarkets such as NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and Don Don Donki.

nutrient_source.title: Atlantic Salmon, Farmed, Raw — USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match to USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 175167: Atlantic salmon, farmed, raw. All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from this approved primary source entry. No estimation or analogy required. Confidence: very high.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167
gi_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
gi_source.source_match_notes: Atlantic salmon fillet (raw) contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g per USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates and produces no measurable postprandial glucose response. Per Project Veritas v2 protocol for zero-carbohydrate fish and seafood: glycemic_index set to null, gi_category set to not_applicable. No GI database lookup required or appropriate.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (59mg per 100g); omega-3 EPA+DHA with modest antihypertensive effect
hypertension.guidance: Atlantic salmon is one of the lowest-sodium protein sources available (59mg/100g raw), well within recommended limits for hypertensive patients; its EPA+DHA content (~1.8g/100g raw) has evidence for modest systolic blood pressure reduction of 1.5–4 mmHg with sustained intake of 2g/day or more via anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory mechanisms.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate content; no postprandial glucose elevation; omega-3 may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw Atlantic salmon contains 0g carbohydrate and has no direct postprandial glucose effect; its high-quality protein (20.4g/100g) supports satiety and lean mass preservation, and sustained omega-3 EPA+DHA intake has evidence for improving insulin sensitivity in type 2 diabetes management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high omega-3 EPA+DHA (~1.8g per 100g raw) — exceeds cardiovascular therapeutic threshold of 1g/day
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Atlantic salmon provides approximately 1.8g EPA+DHA per 100g raw (EPA 0.69g + DHA 1.12g), exceeding the cardiovascular therapeutic threshold of 1g/day; regular consumption of 2–3 portions per week is consistent with AHA dietary guidelines for reducing triglycerides, lowering cardiovascular event risk, and supporting anti-inflammatory vascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EPA+DHA ~1.8g per 100g raw; close to clinical RA trial dose of 2–3g/day
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Clinical RA trials using 2–3g EPA+DHA/day demonstrated reduction in joint stiffness, tender joint count, and disease activity scores (Evidence Grade A); regular salmon consumption of 140–200g portions 2–3 times per week can provide therapeutic EPA+DHA levels from whole food.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Atlantic Salmon Fillet (Raw, Skin-Off) is a raw fatty fish ingredient reference record providing per-100g nutritional values for commercially farmed Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) as sold at Singapore supermarkets including NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, Marks & Spencer Food, and Don Don Donki. Per 100g raw: 208 kcal, 20.4g protein, 13.4g total fat (saturated fat 3.1g; monounsaturated fat 3.8g; polyunsaturated fat 3.9g; EPA 0.69g + DHA 1.12g = approximately 1.8g EPA+DHA total), 0g carbohydrate, 59mg sodium, 490mg potassium, 274mg phosphorus, 36.5mcg selenium, 3.2mcg vitamin B12, and 11.1mcg vitamin D. The omega-3 EPA+DHA content of approximately 1.8g per 100g raw exceeds the cardiovascular therapeutic threshold of 1g/day and approaches the 2–3g/day dose used in rheumatoid arthritis clinical trials. Cooking yield: 100g raw yields approximately 70g cooked (30% moisture loss); when tracking cooked weight, divide by 0.70 to obtain raw equivalent. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g per 100g). Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167. This record is a generic raw ingredient reference — not a prepared dish — intended for use by patients and dietitians tracking home cooking, meal planning, and clinical nutrition calculations.

confidence_reason: All nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167 (Atlantic salmon, farmed, raw) — an approved primary source with exact match to the food described. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are confirmed from this single authoritative entry. No estimation or analogy required.
notes: carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — Atlantic salmon is a pure protein and fat food with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167. sugar_g is naturally zero by the same basis. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as fish contains no dietary fibre. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167. water_g set to 68.5g: USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167 reports water content of 68.50g per 100g for Atlantic salmon farmed raw; this value is sourced directly from the approved primary source. monounsaturated_fat_g (3.8g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (3.9g) are sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167 fatty acid breakdown. Cooking yield note: 100g raw yields approximately 70g cooked (30% moisture loss); divide cooked weight by 0.70 to obtain raw equivalent for tracking purposes. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g per 100g); glycemic_index set to null and gi_category set to not_applicable per Project Veritas v2 protocol for fish and seafood with negligible carbohydrates. This record is a generic raw ingredient reference — not a prepared dish. Multilingual names provided for Singapore market context (Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this food is widely sold and tracked by Singapore consumers and dietitians. Aliases self-check performed: every string in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array including romanized form 'San Wen Yu Pian (Sheng, Qu Pi)'.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.0 (naturally zero, fish contains no fibre), sodium_mg 59.0, saturated_fat_g 3.1, sugar_g 0.0 (naturally zero) — all present as numeric values, plausible and supported by USDA SR Legacy FDC 175167.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to null (not 0) per protocol for zero-carbohydrate foods. gi_category set to 'not_applicable'. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source fully documented with approved source (USDA FoodData Central) confirming 0g carbohydrate basis and explaining not_applicable rationale. Compliant with q4 critical rule for fish and seafood with negligible carbohydrates.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: VT000281 Atlantic Salmon Fillet Raw (Skin-Off) passes all 6 hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. Zero macros (carbohydrate, sugar, fibre, trans_fat) justified as naturally zero or confirmed zero in notes. Multilingual names provided in Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi for Singapore market context. All three required health conditions present with specific clinical guidance and correct 'Type 2 Diabetes' human condition name.
